summary: Structural kernel policy for the metadata-first prose->struct->schema metaschema lane.
authority_sentence: The canonical semantic model is the authoritative middle of the metadata-first transform chain; document projections and operational packet carriers remain downstream.

schema_surfaces.source_registry: Rooted source registry for ingress identity, location, and provenance under a single repo-rooted ref system.
schema_surfaces.structural_units: Intermediate extraction layer carrying unit identity, source provenance, candidate section mapping, and term candidates.
schema_surfaces.canonical_semantic_model: Projection-neutral semantic authority surface for normalized objects, relations, open questions, and projection targets.
schema_surfaces.canonical_document: Derived document-shaped projection model for toc, sections, glossary, open questions, and source maps.
schema_surfaces.projection_artifact_manifest: Derived projection binding contract from semantic targets to emitted artifacts.
schema_surfaces.anchor_catalog: Authoritative catalog for anchor:* refs used in normalized term identity.
schema_surfaces.compatibility_policy: Machine-readable compatibility classification across refs, kinds, anchors, and projection bindings.
schema_surfaces.lane_dependency_contract: Explicit dependency ordering that keeps the metaschema lane upstream of workflow docs and scm.pattern.

workflow_rules: No docgen-only metadata lane may be introduced.
Rooted source resolution precedes structural extraction.
Structural extraction precedes canonical semantic normalization.
Document shaping and other projections occur only downstream of the canonical semantic model.
Workflow docs are explanatory projections and must not back-author model semantics.
The scm.pattern workflow is a downstream operational carrier and must not define semantic authority.
notes: This policy surface binds the upstream metaschema lane only. It does not implement admission, runtime execution, or packet transport behavior.
